_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize SC-01 (open Mace seat) 2026 federal-House batch. June 9 primary already occurred: Ellison withdrew 3/25; Myers/Cunningham/Fulmer lost the primary; Deford advanced to the June 23 Democratic runoff. Evidence-first; pro-choice/gun-control statements documented as FALSE with citations; everything unsupported nulled.

set.office: U.S. Representative SC-01 (2026 R candidate — WITHDREW 3/25/2026, open Mace seat)

profile.background: U.S. Army veteran; University of South Carolina graduate; doctoral student in health administration at MUSC. Ran a biography/process-focused SC-01 campaign ('common sense, accountability, service').

jack-ellison.notes_set: Withdrew from the SC-01 GOP race on 2026-03-25 before the June 9 primary (Holy City Sinner). FEC H6SC01193. No documented positions on any of the 10 RESOLUTE issue areas — only vague 'fiscal responsibility / strong national defense' framing (SOFT). Two unverified aggregator directories list a 'Jack Ellison' running for U.S. Senate (I) in SC 2026; UNCONFIRMED against FEC/Ballotpedia/news — not added here. No church/religion stated.

set.office: U.S. Representative SC-01 (2026 R candidate — LOST June 9 primary, open Mace seat)

profile.background: U.S. Navy combat veteran ('Pirate Hunter'), small-business owner. Self-described 'very moderate conservative... also very libertarian.' Lost the June 9, 2026 SC-01 GOP primary (Honeycutt and Smith advanced to the June 23 runoff).

justin-myers-sc-01.notes_set: Lost the 2026-06-09 SC-01 GOP primary; did not advance to the June 23 Honeycutt-Smith runoff (Ballotpedia News). CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION on life: explicitly pro-choice — 'there's no man that should give a woman what they do with her body... you should have the freedom to choose what you do with your body' (ABC News 4, 9/12/2025). Also backs recreational + medical marijuana and signed the U.S. Term Limits pledge (off-rubric). Other issues only vague slogans (SOFT) -> null. No church stated.

0.note: Pro-choice: 'you should have the freedom to choose what you do with your body' — does not affirm personhood-from-conception as a policy basis.

1.note: Explicitly pro-choice; does not advocate abortion abolition or restrictions.

set.office: U.S. Representative SC-01 (2026 R candidate — LOST June 9 primary; Beaufort County Council District 7)

profile.background: Beaufort County Council member (District 7); youngest person ever elected to that council; former teacher; ~20-year Lowcountry resident. Lost the June 9, 2026 SC-01 GOP primary (5th, 6.68%).

logan-cunningham.notes_set: Lost the 2026-06-09 SC-01 GOP primary — finished 5th district-wide (4,476 votes, 6.68%); Honeycutt and Smith advanced to the June 23 runoff (Island News; Ballotpedia News). Office correction: Beaufort County Council District 7 (not Berkeley/Charleston/Dorchester). Council record (lower taxes, anti-upzoning, green-space referendum, teacher pay) does not map to the 10 RESOLUTE categories. Immigration framing pairs 'secure the border / more Border Patrol' with 'a legal immigration system that works' — too soft/moderated to score the hardline cells. Self-identified Christian. No documented life/marriage/guns/elections/foreign-policy positions -> null.

set.office: U.S. Representative SC-01 (2026 D candidate — June 23 runoff, open Mace seat)

profile.background: U.S. Coast Guard veteran (2007-2012); The Citadel, Charleston School of Law (JD), UNC MBA; former General Counsel for Hilton Head Island. Placed 2nd (~29%) in the June 9, 2026 SC-01 Democratic primary; advanced to the June 23 runoff vs. Nancy Lacore.

mac-deford.notes_set: ACTIVE — advanced to the 2026-06-23 SC-01 Democratic runoff (placed 2nd ~29% on June 9, behind Nancy Lacore; neither hit 50%) (Post and Courier; The Hill). Pledges to introduce legislation to codify a right to abortion; 'women's reproductive freedom is under attack' (campaign site; AOL/Island Packet). Awarded 2024 Gun Sense Candidate Distinction by Moms Demand Action; backs universal background checks, a national digital gun registry, responsible-storage and red-/yellow-flag laws; criticized SC permitless carry (ABC News 4). No cross-party (conservative) deviation found. No church/religion stated.

0.note: Would introduce legislation to codify a right to abortion; 'reproductive freedom under attack' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-abortion-codification; opposite of advocating abolition or restrictions.

0.note: Criticized SC permitless (constitutional) carry: 'our regulations are stricter for liquor than for guns.'

1.note: Supports a national digital gun registry plus red-/yellow-flag laws — opposite of opposing registries and red-flag laws.

set.office: U.S. Representative SC-01 (2026 D candidate — LOST June 9 primary, open Mace seat)

profile.background: First-time candidate; Hilton Head Island waiter for over a decade; ran a working-class, anti-corporate-PAC populist campaign. Lost the June 9, 2026 SC-01 Democratic primary (did not make the June 23 Lacore-Deford runoff).

matt-fulmer.notes_set: Lost the 2026-06-09 SC-01 Democratic primary; only Lacore and Deford advanced to the June 23 runoff (Live 5 News). Documented positions are uniformly progressive: would codify reproductive rights, IVF access, transgender protections, and marriage equality into federal law (BallotReady); supports DREAMer citizenship and modernizing legal immigration; supports ranked-choice voting, single-payer healthcare, the PRO Act, and overturning Citizens United. No conservative cross-party deviation. No documented stance on guns, foreign policy, or Christian liberty -> null. No church stated.

0.note: Would codify reproductive rights into federal law; does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-choice codification; opposite of advocating abolition or restrictions.

2.note: Supports codifying IVF access into federal law — opposite of opposing IVF embryo discard.

0.note: Supports codifying 'marriage equality' (same-sex marriage) into federal law — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Supports same-sex marriage in federal law.

2.note: Supports codifying transgender protections into federal law — opposite of affirming immutable biological sex.

4.note: Backs LGBTQ legal protections; opposite of opposing LGBTQ promotion in public policy.

1.note: Supports citizenship for those brought to the U.S. as children (DREAMers) — opposite of mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens including those who entered as minors.
